---
title: "Lecture 3: HTML Basics & Structure"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "January 28, 2026"
code-annotations: below
format: 
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    code-link: true
    highlight-style: a11y
    chalkboard: true
    transition: slide
    transition-speed: default
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    theme: 
      - ../course-slides-styles.scss
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 3]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[HTML Basics & Structure]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 1 | Unit I: Web Page Designing<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Understand the structure of HTML documents
- Learn how to use basic HTML elements
- Create and structure your first HTML page
:::

::: {.notes}
HTML is the foundation of all web pages. Understanding its structure is crucial before moving to styling or interactivity.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="What is HTML"}

[What is HTML?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

[**HTML** = HyperText Markup Language]{.teal-text}

::: {.body-text-m}
::: {.fragment}
- Standard markup language for creating web pages
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Uses tags to describe and structure content
:::
::: {.fragment}
- NOT a programming language (no logic/loops)
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Current version: **HTML5** (since 2014)
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Clarify that HTML is declarative, not imperative. It describes what things are, not how to do things.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="HTML Document Structure"}

[Basic HTML Document Structure]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2|3-5|6-7|8|9"}
<!DOCTYPE html>                                          # <1>
<html lang="en">                                         # <2>
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">                              # <3>
    <title>My First Web Page</title>                    # <4>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Welcome to Web Technology</h1>                  # <5>
    <p>This is my first HTML page.</p>
</body>
</html>
```
1. Declares this is an HTML5 document
2. Root element with language attribute
3. Metadata and character encoding
4. Page title (shown in browser tab)
5. Visible page content

---

## {data-menu-title="HTML Anatomy"}

[Anatomy of an HTML Element]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ <p class="intro">Hello World</p>    │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────┬─────────┬──────────┬───────────────┐
│ Opening  │ Attribute│ Content  │ Closing Tag   │
│  Tag     │          │          │               │
│ <p       │ class=" │ Hello    │ </p>          │
│  >       │ intro"  │ World    │               │
└──────────┴─────────┴──────────┴───────────────┘
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Element = Opening Tag + Attributes + Content + Closing Tag]{.magenta-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Self-closing Tags"}

[Self-closing (Void) Elements]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

Some HTML elements don't need closing tags:

::: {.body-text-m}
```html
<img src="image.jpg" alt="Description" />

<br />    <!--Line break-->

<hr />    <!--Horizontal rule-->

<input type="text" />

<meta charset="UTF-8" />
```
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[These don't have content, just attributes]{.teal-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Common HTML Tags"}

[Common HTML Tags]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
[**Structural**]{.teal-text}

::: {.body-text-m}
- `<h1>` to `<h6>`: Headings
- `<p>`: Paragraph
- `<div>`: Container
- `<section>`: Section
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
[**Content**]{.teal-text}

::: {.body-text-m}
- `<a>`: Hyperlink
- `<img>`: Image
- `<ul>`, `<ol>`, `<li>`: Lists
- `<table>`: Tabular data
:::
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="HTML Attributes"}

[HTML Attributes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Attributes provide additional information about elements:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
<a href="https://example.com" target="_blank">Link</a>

<img src="logo.png" alt="Company Logo" width="200" height="100">

<div id="header" class="container main-section">Content</div>
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
[**Common attributes**: `id`, `class`, `src`, `href`, `alt`, `title`, `style`]{.teal-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Heading Tags"}

[Heading Hierarchy]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
```html
<h1>Page Title</h1>        <!-- Use ONLY once -->
<h2>Section 1</h2>
<h3>Subsection 1.1</h3>
<h4>Subsection 1.1.1</h4>
<h5>Minor heading</h5>
<h6>Smallest heading</h6>
```
:::

::: {.incremental .body-text-m .topbr}
✓ Use `<h1>` only once per page
✓ Follow hierarchical order (no jumping from h1 to h3)
✓ Improves SEO and accessibility
:::

::: {.notes}
Heading hierarchy is important for both search engines and screen readers for people with visual impairments.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Paragraphs and Text"}

[Paragraphs & Text Formatting]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
```html
<p>This is a paragraph. Text wraps naturally.</p>

<strong>Bold text (important)</strong>
<b>Also bold (presentation)</b>

<em>Italic text (emphasis)</em>
<i>Also italic (presentation)</i>

<u>Underlined text</u>

<mark>Highlighted text</mark>

<small>Smaller text</small>
```
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-s .center-text .topbr}
Prefer semantic tags (`<strong>`, `<em>`) over presentation tags (`<b>`, `<i>`)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="HTML Nesting"}

[Proper HTML Nesting]{.slide-title}

<hr>

✅ [**Correct**]{.teal-text}

```html
<div>
    <p>This is <strong>correct</strong> nesting.</p>
</div>
```

❌ [**Incorrect**]{.magenta-text}

```html
<div>
    <p>This is <strong>incorrect nesting.</p>
    </strong>
</div>
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
Always close tags in reverse order of opening: Last opened, first closed
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Creating Your First Page"}

[Creating Your First HTML Page]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
1. Open a text editor (VS Code, Notepad++)
2. Type the HTML structure
3. Save as `.html` file
4. Open in a web browser
5. See your creation!
:::

::: {.fragment}
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>My First Page</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Welcome!</h1>
    <p>This is my first webpage.</p>
</body>
</html>
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Validation"}

[HTML Validation]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

Always validate your HTML:

- **[W3C Validator](https://validator.w3.org/)** - Official HTML validator
- Check for syntax errors
- Ensure standards compliance
- Catch accessibility issues

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
Validation prevents browsers from having to guess your intentions
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Quick Practice"}

[Quick Practice Exercise]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Create an HTML page with:

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
1. A main heading with your name
2. A paragraph about yourself
3. A `<em>` or `<strong>` somewhere
4. A subheading "My Interests"
5. A list of your interests (use `<ul>`)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Resources"}

[Resources & References]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- [MDN: HTML - Hypertext Markup Language](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML)
- [W3C: HTML Standard](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/)
- [W3Schools: HTML Tutorial](https://www.w3schools.com/html/)
- [Course content: Week 1](/content/week-01.qmd)
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 4 - HTML Grouping: Div & Span
</div>
</div>
